title: In the dropdown, click Cancel Subscription.

sections.heading: Link an AWS Billing Account to MongoDB Atlas
sections.body:
To link your AWS (Amazon Web Services) Billing Account to a MongoDB Atlas organization:

> **Note:** You must resolve all outstanding or failed invoices in your Atlas
> organization before linking it to your cloud service Billing Account.

If you are already
signed in to an existing MongoDB Atlas account,
you are automatically redirected to the
Select an Organization to Link to Your AWS Billing Account
page on the MongoDB Cloud Atlas website
when you click Set Up Your Account.

If you are not already signed in to an existing
MongoDB Atlas account, you are prompted to sign in to
a MongoDB Atlas account. Upon successful sign-in,
you are automatically redirected to the
Select an Organization to Link
to Your AWS Billing Account
page.

> **Note:** If you are unable to complete your registration,
> you can return to this step by navigating to the
> Manage Subscriptions page in the AWS Marketplace.

On the Select an Organization to Link to Your AWS Billing Account page,
click the Link button in the Actions
column of the organization you wish to link your AWS account to.
You are redirected to the Billing overview page
of the organization that you are linking your
AWS account to.

If you do not have any organizations available to link your AWS account to,
[Create an Atlas organization](create-organization).

> **Important:** If the Link button for a particular organization
> is disabled,
> the organization is unable to be linked to your AWS account.
> To see why you are unable to link a particular organization,
> mouse over the disabled Link button to display
> a tooltip with more information.

In the bottom left-hand corner of the Billing page,
a pop-up notifying you that your organization
is being synced with your AWS account is displayed.
Additionally, the
Payment Method field of the Billing page
remains empty until the sync is complete.

Upon successful sync,
you will see *AWS Marketplace Subscription* is now
listed in the Payment Method field.

> **Note:** When you have an AWS (Amazon Web Services) Marketplace Subscription:
>
> - You cannot add a credit card as a backup payment.
>
> - Atlas disables the Edit button in the
>   Payment Method field.
>
> To remove the *AWS Marketplace Subscription* payment method,
> you must [unlink your AWS Billing Account from MongoDB](unlink-aws-billing-account).

sections.heading: Unlink an AWS Billing Account from MongoDB Atlas
sections.body:
To unlink your AWS Billing Account from MongoDB:

If you do not already have an AWS account,
[create a new AWS account](https://aws.amazon.com/premiumsupport/knowledge-center/create-and-activate-aws-account/).

The information for the selected subscription is displayed.

A dropdown with available actions is displayed.

> **Important:** Cancelling your subscription unlinks your AWS (Amazon Web Services) billing account
> from MongoDB but does **not** delete your resources or data. You
> will continue to incur charges for your MongoDB resources and data
> after you unlink your AWS billing account. To manage billing
> through MongoDB, see [Billing Cost Explorer](atlas-billing). To delete your
> Atlas resources and data, see [delete](delete-atlas-account).

A modal asking you if you are sure you want to cancel your subscription
displays. If you wish to cancel your subscription,
type "confirm" and press Yes, cancel subscription.
Upon successful cancellation of your subscription,
a green banner displays at the top of the
Manage Subscriptions page.

If you do not wish to cancel, click No, don't cancel.
